front: Happening: American artist who coined the term 'happenings' to describe participatory performance events involving audiences as participants
work: Happenings (as a form) / 18 Happenings in 6 Parts (1959)

front: Happening: 18 Happenings in 6 Parts (1959) — presented at the Reuben Gallery and documented by Michael Kirby
work: Happenings (as a form) / 18 Happenings in 6 Parts (1959)

front: Happening: coined a term defined as 'a game, an adventure, a number of activities engaged in by participants for the sake of playing'
work: Happenings (as a form) / 18 Happenings in 6 Parts (1959)

front: Happening: Words — audience members tear fragments from two rolls of cloth printed with random texts
work: Happenings (as a form) / 18 Happenings in 6 Parts (1959)

front: Artist: essay 'The Legacy of Jackson Pollock' introduced both 'environments' and 'happenings'
Artist: wrote 'The Legacy of Jackson Pollock' while teaching at Rutgers
Artist: 2001 essay anthology The Blurring of Art and Life collects his writings, including his foundational essay on Jackson Pollock's legacy
Artist: called for concrete art made of 'paint, chairs, food, electric and neon lights, smoke, water, old socks, a dog, and movies'
Happening: Eat — took place in a cave in the Bronx; a girl fried bananas while visitors were offered red or white wine
Happening: Household — women lick strawberry jam off the hood of a burning car; photographed by Sol Goldberg at Cornell
Happening: several works involve covering bodies or cars with jam
Installation: coined the term 'environments' for transformed gallery spaces — considered a precursor to installation art
Installation: Yard — car tires filled the space behind the Martha Jackson Gallery
Artist: studied under composer John Cage
Artist: taught Claes Oldenburg; happenings inspired Oldenburg's practice
Artist: member of Fluxus, alongside Yoko Ono and Nam June Paik
Artist: essay 'Art Which Can't Be Art' argues that brushing one's teeth can be an artistic practice
Artist: described in Pablo Helguera's Education for Socially Engaged Art as influential to socially engaged art in the United States
